1.content:
Primary Characters:
• Jamie Lincoln (You): An 18-year-old boy, you recently graduated from High School with plans to attend Penn State. Your college plans were destroyed by the end of the world.
• Ashley Saunders: An 18-year-old girl; a former High School classmate of yours, spent High School tormenting you. She comes from a wealthy local family, but she’s not spoiled. She is also much smarter than she portrays herself to be.
• Grace Anderson: 24 years old; a Lieutenant in the US Army, her enlistment was cut short by the depth of her boyfriend. She left the Army a few months before the apocalypse to raise her child as a single mother.
• Caleb Anderson: Grace’s 4-year-old son.

Secondary Characters:
• Emma Lincoln: Your 15-year-old sister
• Eric Sullivan – 18 years old; was, until recently, Ashley’s boyfriend.
• Jacob Amherst: Grace's recently deceased boyfriend, and Caleb's father. He worked for FEMA's regional office in Philadelphia.

How Did The World End?
1.title: Meet the Cast

11.content: The world has been destroyed in a nuclear war. You struggle to survive in the radiation-soaked wasteland that was once America.
11.title: Nuclear War

111.content:
No one really knows what caused the bombs to fall; all everyone remembered from that day was reports of missiles being launched from Russia and China. 3.5 billion people, slightly under half the world’s population, was killed in an instant. Those that survived found themselves struggling to survive in an irradiated wasteland.

Key Points:
• Emma is missing
• Your parents are dead

The Story Begins

It started out as a ordinary day; the sun was shining, and the birds were singing. No one had any idea that things were about to take a sudden turn for the worst. I was sitting in the food court at the Ross Park Mall, minding my own business as I ate my lunch. My parents were at a wedding in Chicago, and Emma was spending the day with friends.

I was watching an episode of The Mandalorian on my phone when someone bumped into me, spilling my soda on my lap in the process. Jumping to my feet, I spun around and saw Ashley Sullivan and her boyfriend Eric standing a few feet from me. Ashley looked apologetic, while Eric burst out laughing when he saw that my pants were wet, “Geez Jamie; don’t you know that’s what bathrooms are for?”

I was tempted to lash out, but quickly realized that wouldn’t do me any good. Looking at the two of them, I said, “Why don’t you guys just watch where you’re going next time.”

Eric walked off in a huff, leaving Ashley standing there with the same look on her face. Looking at me as she grabbed some napkins, she said, “Sorry about that, Jamie; I guess I was too focused on my phone.”

Taking the napkins, I said, “Forgive me if I don’t accept your apology.”

You’re probably thinking that was rude of me; but Ashley and her friends had made my life hell all through High School. Her apology meant nothing to me. Frowning a bit, Ashley said, “We graduated two months ago, Jamie; isn’t it about time you forgave me?”

“You made my life hell for 4 years; even though I never did anything to you.” Sighing, I said, “So why should I ever forgive you?”

Ashley looked as though she was about to respond, only to be interrupted by a sudden blinding light outside. We barely had time to cover our eyes before we, along with everything and everyone around us, was blown off our feet.
111.title: Judgement Day

1111.content:
I couldn’t remember much of what happened after the blast threw Ashley and me across the food court. That made things even more confusing when I opened my eyes to find myself surrounded by utter chaos.

I couldn’t really move my head much, but I could tell that I was no longer inside the mall. It looked like I was lying on a gurney inside of a tent or someplace like that. Looking to my right, I was surprised to see Ashley resting her by my knees; holding my hand in hers while her right arm was in a sling.

Finding it difficult to move my body, I groaned just loud enough for her to hear me. Shooting up in her seat, wincing in pain, Ashley said, “Thank God; you’re awake!”

I couldn’t, for the life of me, think of any reason why Ashley would be by my bedside. “What’s going on, Ashley?” Looking at her, I said, “What are you doing here?”

“There was this huge explosion a few miles away; the blast threw us across the room and all but destroyed the food court. I woke up first; found you bleeding pretty badly from a gash on your leg, I managed to slow the bleeding enough that you wouldn’t bleed out.” Brushing away a stray hair, she said, “I have to admit, I was pretty worried when you didn’t wake up. Thankfully some EMTs found us not long after.”

My gaze returning to the sling on her arm, I said, “What happened to your arm?”

“They said I fractured my arm in two places, which is nothing compared to a few other people I’ve seen around here.”

“Don’t take this the wrong way, Ashley; but why are you here with me? Shouldn’t you be off somewhere with Eric?”

A look of sadness flashed across Ashley’s face, “Eric’s still missing; the area where I saw him last is pretty much destroyed.”
Looking at me, Ashley said, “So while I wait for news about him, I thought I’d stay with the only other person here that I know.”

“Forgive me if I have a tough time accepting that.”

“Look, I know I made your life hell during High School; but I’m not a monster.” Sighing, Ashley said, “With everything going on, I thought we could both use a familiar face.”

Choosing to table that topic for the time being, I said, “Do they know what kind of explosion it was?”

“Not really; some people are saying it was a gas main explosion, some say it was a homemade bomb.” Turning serious, Ashley said, “I overheard one of the soldiers talking earlier – she mentioned something a possible nuclear weapon.”
1111.title: Waking Up

11111.content:
If I’d had any liquid in my mouth, I would have done a spat it out all over Ashley. Looking at her, I said, “Did you say nuclear?”

“That’s what I heard; but who knows if it’s true.” Taking a deep breath, Ashley said, “To tell you the truth, I don’t think they have a clue what’s going on.”

I sat there for a moment, letting everything seep in. Once that was finished, I said, “You mentioned something about Eric still missing?”

“Yeah; after bumping into you, we started towards the Mall exit.” Wiping a stray tear, Ashley said, “I felt terrible about what happened; so, I turned back to help you. The last time I saw Eric, he was walking in the opposite direction. Suddenly the explosion happened, and that section of the mall was gone.”

I didn’t particularly care for Eric; but if what Ashley said was true, he didn’t deserve to die like that. Seeing the tears in her eyes, I took her free hand and said, “I’m sure he’ll be okay.”

“I know you’re just saying that to be nice, but I appreciate it.” Ashley flashed a weak smile, “Until Eric’s found, dead or alive, I’m going stay right here with you.”
11111.title: Reactions

111111.content:
Ashley was true to her word, staying close by as the day worn on. After a while I could tell she was getting tired; so, being the gentleman that I was, I made some room in the bed for her. “Here, lie down next to me.”

“If it’s all the same to you, I’d rather not.”

Under normal circumstances, I would understand why Ashley would say that; but these weren’t normal circumstances. “Look; I promise it won’t be weird or anything.” Patting the mattress, I said, “This bed’s a hell of a lot more comfortable than that chair you’re sitting in.”

Ashley glanced from the bed to the chair; eventually saying, “I suppose you’re right.”

Before Ashley could even get up from the stair, a dozen people in hazmat suits entered the tent. “Everyone needs to leave now! The winds have shifted; this whole area will be covered in radioactive fallout within the next 15 minutes.”

The tent descended into chaos as everyone started panicking. I quickly got out of bed, concussion be damned, and grabbed Ashley’s hand as I ran for the door. Once we were through the chaos and outside the tent, Ashley stopped me, “Where are we going, Jamie?”

If watching TV and movies had taught me anything, it was that the safest place you could to avoid radiation was underground. Looking at Ashley, I said, “We have to get to the parking garage; get as far underground as we can.”

Ashley nodded in understanding as the two of us ran in the direction of the parking garage a ¼ mile away. Everywhere we went there was chaos; people scrambling in every conceivable direction. The only person who had the same idea was an older woman carrying a young boy in her arms.

The four of us ran through the crowds, eventually making it out of the parking lot and into the streets. It was here that I caught my first glimpse of the destruction – buildings on fire, cars overturned, and an assortment of dead bodies.

We eventually made it a doorway on the side of the garage; only to find that it was locked. Knowing our time was running out, I was about to search for another entrance when I had the *pop* *pop* of a gun. Turning back, I saw the woman pointing a pistol at what remained of the door. Looking at me, the woman said, “Come on, don’t just stand there.”

The four of us ran through the door and down the stairs. Once we reached the bottom level, we exited into the parking garage. We stuck together as we looked for a place to lie low; eventually finding a small maintenance room on the far end.

Once inside the room, I helped the woman barricade the door. After barricading it to her satisfaction, the woman returned to the young boy. Holding him in her arms, she said, “I guess we’re going to be here a while. My name’s Grace; this is my son Caleb.”

“It’s nice to meet you; I’m Jamie.” Gesturing to Ashley, I said, “That’s Ashley.”
111111.title: Later On

12.content: It's hard to believe there was once a time where the dead stayed dead. No one knows how it happened, but something has caused the dead to rise again.
12.title: Zombie Apocalypse

121.content:
My story begins two weeks after the plague began spreading across the globe; my family and I had evacuated to a refugee camp at Heinz Field. I was in line with my sister Emma, waiting to get something to eat, when an announcement came over the loudspeaker – another group of survivors was arriving.

Normally I would’ve gone to watch the group enter, in the hopes of seeing someone I knew, but my hunger took precedence. Little did I know my decision to stay in line would save my life. Emma and I were about to sit down for lunch when a siren started blaring – the camp had been breached.

I quickly grabbed Emma’s hand as we ran through the crowd to safety. We soon got separated in the chaos, her panicked screams still echoing in my head. I looked everywhere for her but to no avail. I was on the verge of giving up when I heard a scream nearby.

Hoping it was Emma, I ran in the direction of the scream. Rounding the corner of a tent, I saw a young girl struggling to fend off a zombie. Jumping into action, I pulled the zombie off the girl and smashed its head against the pavement.

Turning to check on the girl, I stopped in my tracks when I realized who it – Ashley Sullivan; a classmate from High School and a frequent tormentor of mine. Looking back at the zombie, I saw that it was her boyfriend Eric. Judging from the bite mark on his arm, he must’ve been infected without anyone knowing.

Turning my attention back to Ashley, I reached out for her hand only to have her recoil. As memories of her bullying me for 4 years played in my head, I said, “Look; you don’t like me, and I certainly don’t like you. But I’ll be dammed if I’m going to die here today.”

Ashley considered her options before grabbing my hand. Staying close, the two of us ran through the chaos in search of safety. It took a while, what with avoiding the undead and all, but we eventually made our way to the camp gate.

We were about to leave when I suddenly turned to look back at the camp. My entire family was in there somewhere and I wasn’t even sure they were alive. I started taking a step back inside, only for Ashley to grab my hand, “Jamie, you can’t go back in there.”

Spinning around, I said, “I can’t leave without my family.”

“My family is in there too, Jamie; but there’s nothing we can do about that right now.” Looking at me, she said, “Right now we have to worry about keeping ourselves alive.”

I looked back and forth between Ashley and the camp; struggling to decide what I should do next. After giving it a few moments of thought, I ______.
121.title: The Rise of the Undead

1211.content:
As painful as it was for me to admit, deep down I knew that Ashley was right. We were both safe for the moment and going back into the chaos would only jeopardize that. After taking one last look back into the camp, with smoke from fires billowing into the sky, I turned and walked off.

There was chaos and screams all around us; leaving Ashley and me to wonder where we should go next. Walking around a corner, we stopped in our tracks when we saw a zombie feasting on the deceased body of a young woman in her 20s. The look of terror in her eyes was all I needed to know that her death was excruciating.

The zombie must’ve sensed us, because it looked up and saw us standing a few feet away. I grabbed Ashley’s hand and started running in the opposite direction as it gave chase. Looking for somewhere to hide, I spotted a nearby building and made a beeline for it.

Once there, I discovered the door was barricaded from the inside. Looking through the window, I saw someone moving inside but wasn’t sure if they were alive or dead. With Ashley clutching my arm, I banged on the window in a desperate attempt to get the person’s attention. I quickly realized they were alive when they spun around and pointed a rifle at me.

“Please; you must let us in.” banging on the window again, I said, “We’re going to die out here if you don’t.”

The figure stepped forward into the light, revealing itself to be a woman. Opening the door just enough to peek out, she said, “How do I know you haven’t been bitten?”

“We haven’t, I assure you.” Glancing back at the approaching horde, I said, “Please just let us in.”

The woman hesitated for a few seconds until she started to quickly move things away from the door. Once the door was clear, Ashley and I quickly pushed our way inside just as the horde was about to reach us. Ashley clung to me while the woman hurriedly barricaded the door once more.

Satisfied that the door would hold, the woman spun around and pointed the rifle at us once more. “You can stay here until they leave; but if you’ve been bitten, I won’t hesitate to put you down.”

“We haven’t been bitten; I assure you.” Looking at the woman, Ashley said, “My zombie boyfriend was about to bite me, but Jamie saved me before he could.”

Upon hearing that the woman shifted the rifle towards Ashley, “We’re going into that office over there; I’m going to make sure you’re telling the truth.” Once more pointing the rifle at me, the woman said, “You’re going to stay out here; don’t do anything stupid or I’ll kill you both.”

I thought the woman’s threat was a bit much, but I wasn’t going to argue with someone pointing a gun at my head. Looking at me as she walked towards the next room, Ashley said, “I’ll be okay, Jamie.”

Sitting down after the door closed, I realized just how different things were now – I was concerned for Ashley’s safety. I was about to let my guard down when I heard a noise coming from another nearby room. Reaching for something to protect myself, I cautiously approached the door just as young boy poked his head out.

Seeing the scissors in my hand, the boy stepped back, “Where’s my mommy?”

Dropping the scissors on the ground, I looked at the kid. I quickly realized he must’ve been that woman’s kid; now her behavior made perfect sense. Kneeling down, I said, “She’s in the next room with a friend of mine. My name’s Jamie, what’s yours?”

“C-Caleb.”

Reaching my left hand out, I said, “It’s nice to meet you, Caleb.”
1211.title: Escape with Ashley

12111.content:
Ashley and the woman were in the other room for about 15 minutes. I could hear muffled talking but couldn’t hear what was being said.

Caleb was playing nearby, keeping a wary eye on me. I was about to go talk to him when the door opened; Ashley stepped out with the woman behind her.

Walking over to Ashley, I said, “Are you okay?”

“Yeah, I’m fine.” Sighing, she said, “Grace just wanted to make sure I wasn’t hiding anything.”

Approaching the woman, Grace, I said, “Are you satisfied that we’re not a threat to you?”

Shaking her head, Grace said, “Just because neither of you was bitten doesn’t mean you’re not a threat.” Stepping next to Caleb, she said, “You do anything I don’t like; I won’t hesitate to throw either of you outside.”

Stepping closer to Ashley, who surprisingly leaned against me, I said, “Neither of us is a threat to you or Caleb.” Pausing, I said, “The real threat is out there.”

************


The four of us settled in for the evening; getting to know each other while keeping tabs on the situation outside. As it turns out, Grace had served for 6 years in the Army. “I wanted to make the Army my career; but then my boyfriend died and left me as a single parent.”

Nibbling on a bag of vending machine chips, Ashley said, “I suppose you’ll have an advantage in this new world; what with your weapons experience.”

“I suppose so; just never figured I’d be fighting zombies.” Looking at us, Grace said, “What about you two? How long have you been dating?”

Ashley and I stared at each other for a moment. Eventually I turned to Grace and said, “Oh we’re not a couple.”

“Huh; with how you are acting around each other, I figured you were dating.”

“Trust me; Ashley’s the last person on Earth that I’d ever consider dating.” Sighing, I said, “She made my life hell throughout High School.”

“If you think your life was hell then you clearly don’t remember how things were before I hooked up with Eric.”

I remembered how things were between them; and it wasn’t pretty – his actions bordered on stalking. “I can’t believe I’m saying this, but you could have done better then him.” Groaning, I said, “I mean the guy was caught watching you in the locker room, but you still hooked up with him.”

“Yeah, well hindsight is 20/20.” Sighing, Ashley said, “Like countless woman before me, I thought I could change him.”

Changing the subject, Grace said, “What do you two have planned for tomorrow?”

“I have to find out what happened to the rest of my family; after that, I don’t know.” Turning to Ashley, I said, “I suppose it wouldn’t hurt if we stuck together for the time being.”

“Well you did save my life, so I suppose I owe it to you to help you find your folks.” Looking down, Ashley said, “Perhaps it’ll help make up for High School.”

I wasn’t sure about that, but I didn’t say anything. Turning to Grace, I said, “What about you and Caleb?”

“I’m going to get as far from here as I can.” Sighing, she said, “You two are welcome to tag along.”

Ashley and I looked at each other for a moment. After she nodded in agreement, I turned to Grace and said, “We’ll stick with you until I find out what happened to my family.”

***********


Grace took the first watch while the rest of us got some sleep. We improvised a sleeping area in one of the offices; Caleb got the couch while Ashley and I got the floor.

I was about to close my eyes when I felt someone climbing under the blanket with me. Turning over, I was surprised to see Ashley looking at me.

Before I could say anything, she said “Don’t read too much into it; this is simply me feeling safe with you.”

“Okay.” Smiling, I said, “Goodnight Ashley.”
12111.title: Settling In for the Night

121111.content:
We didn’t leave the safety of the shelter until late the next morning; and that was only after Grace made sure nothing could get to Caleb. The three of us cautiously ventured towards the main gate into the camp; hiding to avoid the occasional zombie.

Once we were safely inside the camp, Grace approached the bodies of two deceased soldiers. Grabbing their weapons, she handed one to myself and Ashley. “Normally I wouldn’t recommend given weapons to someone who isn’t trained; but under the circumstances I don’t see much of a choice.” Pointing to her left, Grace said, “I’m heading that way to find transportation; let’s meet back here in an hour.”

After parting ways with Grace, Ashley and I started walking through the camp towards the area where we’d first met. The whole area was eerily quiet, save for the sounds of zombies groaning in the distance. We were making some decent progress until Ashley tripped and fell to the ground.

Reaching down to help her up, I stopped when I noticed some bruising on her arms – bruising I hadn’t noticed the day before
Helping her up, I said, “Where are those bruises from?”

Pulling out of my grasp, getting defensive, Ashley said, “It’s none of your business, Jamie.”

I stood there as Ashley walked ahead of me; wondering to myself why she was suddenly so defensive. I went to catch up as Ashley walked around one of the nearby tents; only starting to run once I heard what sounded like grunting. Thinking she was in danger; I ran around the corner to find her kicking and bludgeoning the remains of a deceased individual.

I was about to stop her, thinking she was disrespecting the dead, when I realized the body belonged to Eric. After watching her lash out against Eric’s body, I stepped forward and stopped her. “Whoa, Ashley stop! What the hell are you doing?”

Giving Eric’s head one last kick, Ashley turned to me and said, “Just making sure that asshole gets what he deserves.”

Confused, I said, “What are you talking about?”

Showing me the bruises on her arm, and some on her back, Ashley said, “You want to know where I got these bruises from?” pointing to Eric, she said, “That asshole was abusive to me the entire time we were dating.”

Stunned, I said, “What?”

Starting to tear up, Ashley said, “Do you remember Junior year; when I had to wear a cast on my leg for 2 months?” catching her breath, she continued, “I told everyone I broke my leg skiing, but the truth was Eric pushed me down the stairs.”

To say I was stunned would be an understatement, “Why would you stay with someone like that?”

“Please; do you know how many girls wind up dead for leaving guys like Eric?” looking at me, Ashley said, “I’ll give you a clue: it’s a lot.”

I could see that this was a sensitive topic for Ashley; judging by the tears streaming down her face and her skin turning flush. I knew we were in a life-threatening situation, but I knew what I had to do. Embracing Ashley, I said, “You’re safe now, Ashley; I promise I’ll never let anything happen to you.”

Ashley struggled for a few seconds, clearly uncomfortable with physical contact. Eventually she stopped and I could feel her start to relax as she clung to me. Being only an inch or two shorter than me, Ashley didn’t have to crane her neck as she looked up at me. She was on the verge of saying something when a nearby noise caught our attention.

Breaking away from Ashley, I ran in the direction of the noise – hoping against all odds that it was Emma. Running through the
maze of tents, I rounded a corner and saw someone standing nearby. Their back was facing me, but I quickly realized who it was. “Mom!”

Ecstatic, I started running towards my Mom; only to stop when she turned in my direction. Standing in front of me was something that had, at one point, been my mother. But that wasn’t the case anymore: the bite mark on her shoulder, and the glossed-over look in her eyes made that clear. She was a zombie.
121111.title: The Next Day

1211111.content:
I stood frozen in place as I stared at what had once been my mother. I started getting emotional the longer I stared at her; to the point where I didn’t even react as she started lumbering towards me.

Her groans grew louder as she got closer until she was mere inches from me. I snapped out of my daze at that point, only to realize I was screwed. Or I was until I heard a grunt to my left; followed by Ashley shoving me out of the way before knocking my mother to the ground.

I watched from a few feet away as Ashley took out her gun, pointed it at my mom’s head, and pulled the trigger. After checking to ensure she was dead, Ashley ran over to me, “Jamie! Jamie, are you okay?”

I looked at Ashley for a second, too stunned to say anything. Eventually I managed to say, “I-I’m fine.”

“Thank God.” Grabbing my hand, Ashley said, “Come on, we have to go; no doubt there’s more of those things coming.”

I slowly got to my feet and followed Ashley away from the scene; the last memory of my mother was seeing her brains splattered on the pavement. We kept running until we were certain nothing was following us. It was at that point that my legs gave out and I collapsed to the ground.

Ashley kept running for about 15 feet before realizing I wasn’t behind her. Running back to me, she dropped to her knees and said, “Jamie, we have to keep moving.”

“Ashley, I don’t think I can.” Tearing up, I said, “My mom’s dead; in all likelihood my Dad and sister are as well.”

“I’m not leaving you here to die, Jamie; I’ve lost too many people already.” Dragging me into a nearby tent, she said, “You saved my life, now let me save yours.”

Once the two of us were safe inside the tent, we sat on the ground alongside a gurney. I don’t know what came over me at that moment, but something compelled me to pull Ashley in for a kiss. I had expected her to pull away; but, after a few seconds, she started to reciprocate. The two of us kissed for what seemed like an eternity, until I heard someone yelling outside.

Breaking the kiss, leaving Ashley confused, I got to my feet and walked to the tent entrance. Stepping outside, I saw Grace standing a short distance away. “Ashley! Jamie! Where are you?”

To get her attention, I yelled out, “Grace!”

Grace ran towards me, a look of relief in her eyes. Upon reaching us, she said, “I heard a gunshot and assumed the worst.” Seeing the look on my face, she said, “What happened?”

“We, uh, ran into my mom – she was a zombie.” Tearing up again, I said, “Ash-Ashley had to put her down.”

As she hugged me, Grace turned to Ashley and said, “That must’ve been difficult for you.”

Shaking her head, Ashley said, “I did what had to be done.”

“I found an RV in the parking lot; it’s got nearly a full tank of gas.” Looking at us, Grace said, “I suggest we gather what supplies we can; including weapons, ammo and food.”

*************************************


It wasn’t easy but the three of us managed to fill the RV with whatever supplies we could find. While Ashley sat at the table with me, Grace drove through the barriers blocking the exit. After stopping to get Caleb and the rest of our things, the five of us piled into the RV and left.

As we drove through the streets, Ashley took my hand and said, “Your Dad and Emma are still out there, Jamie; I know we’ll find them eventually.”
1211111.title: A Heart-wrenching Discovery

121112.content:
Everyone was focused on Grace after she stopped talking; surprised by her risky suggestion. “You can’t be serious, Grace.” Looking at her, I said, “There are dozens of zombies between us and the RV; we’ll never make it.”

“Look, I know it’s not ideal; but who knows what the situation will be tonight.” Grabbing her rifle, Grace said, “We know how many there are right now; but all that noise will start attracting more the longer we wait.”

I stopped to consider Grace’s argument and slowly realized she was right. I reached for my rifle, only for Ashley to look at me, “I can’t let you do this, Jamie.”

Heading to the ladder, I said, “We have no choice, Ash; all of our supplies are in the RV.” Looking at her, I said, “Stay here and
keep Caleb safe; Grace and I shouldn’t be long.”

Grace looked up at the perch as I reached the bottom, “Ashley; if something happens and we don’t make it back, wait until tomorrow before making your move. Okay?”

“Okay.” Looking down at us, Ashley said, “Good luck.”

After that Grace and I proceeded silently back towards the farmhouse; stopping behind a large boulder just inside the tree line to get a look at the situation. There were about a dozen zombies spread out around the outside of the house, with an unknown number inside. The area around the RV was clear for the moment.

Looking at Grace, I said, “What’s the plan?”

Checking her rifle one last time, Grace said, “I’ll draw them away while you head for the RV.” Looking at me, she said, “Wait for me at the end of the driveway.”

“Okay.”

With that, Grace fired off a few shots to draw the attention of the zombies. The plan worked; I watched dozens of them start to slowly follow her. I waited until the coast was clear before I ran towards the RV; whacking the skull of a straggler as I passed.

Approaching the RV, I was relieved to see that it was empty. Opening the door, I climbed inside and sat down at the wheel. It took a few tries to get the RV started, but I soon had it running. Once that was accomplished, I turned around and headed towards the road.

Heading down the driveway, I saw Grace running ahead of the herd a few hundred foot away. Slowing down slightly, I rolled down the window and yelled, “Grace!”

Grace turned to look at me; briefly considering her options before turning in my direction. My heart skipped a beat as she stumbled; but I was relieved when she got up and started running towards me once more. Once she was close enough, I slowed down just enough for her to open the door and climb inside.

Plopping herself down on the couch, catching her breath, Grace said, “That...was…fun.”

Approaching the main road, I said, “Now we just have to meet up with Ashley and Caleb; and then we’ll be set.”
121112.title: We Should Go Now

1211121.content:
We drove for a good mile before coming to a stop on the side of the road. After turning the engine off, I spun around to look at Grace. “I think we lost them; if we hurry, we can rescue Caleb and Ashley before the zombies catch up.”

“You’re going to have to get them yourself, Jamie.” Gesturing to her swollen ankle, Grace said, “I twisted my ankle when I fell.”

I knew better than to argue with Grace; so, after making sure she was comfortable, I started running through the woods back to the hunter’s perch. The perch was only a ½ mile from the road, so it didn’t take me long to get back.

Approaching the perch, I was about to start climbing when I heard a rifle cock and saw the muzzle poke out over the edge. “Ash,
it’s me!”

Peering down over the edge, looking relieved, Ashley said, “Don’t sneak up like that; I almost blew your head off.”

Climbing up the ladder, I said, “I can apologize later; but right now, we’ve got to move.”

Without hesitating, Ashley took Caleb in her arms and started climbing down the ladder after me. I was about to start walking when Ashley’s hand pulled me back; before I could say anything, she gave me a kiss on the lips and said, “I’m glad you’re okay.”

After that, the three of us made our way back to the RV; arriving just in time to see the herd cresting the hill a short distance away.
Once everyone was situated, I started the engine and continued down the road.
1211121.title: Meeting Up with Ashley & Caleb

12111211.content:
We drove for an hour before deciding it was time to take a break. Once again, I pulled off into the parking lot of an abandoned gas station. I joined the others at the table while Grace made us sandwiches.

I was about to take a bite of mine when I heard screaming in the distance. Rushing up to the front I saw a small group, including a young girl a few years older than Caleb, fleeing from a herd of zombies. Grace and Ashley had joined me by that point, watching as the group got closer.

Taking one look at them, Ashley turned to me and said, “We have to help them.”

Normally I would have been the first one to rush out and help; but things were different now. “We can’t help them, Ash; we barely have enough supplies for the four of us. How do you expect us to feed three more?”

Pointing out the window, Ashley said, “We can’t just leave them here to die, Jamie.”

“Look, I don’t like it any more than you do; but this is what the world’s come to.” Sitting down in the driver’s seat, I said, “My only concern right now is keeping the four of us safe; I’m not going to jeopardize that.”

Turning to start the engine, I stopped the moment I heard Grace yell, “Ashley, stop!”

I spun around just in time to see Ashley rush out the door with a rifle in her hands. I watched from the seat as she ran to the group barely 50 feet away; taking down several zombies as she got close. I couldn’t hear what she was saying, but I watched as she picked the little girl up in her arms and started running back towards the RV – the man and the woman not far behind.

Everything looked like it was going to turn out fine, until the woman tripped and fell to the ground. Ashley started to run back only to be stopped by the man as he ran back towards the woman. All I could do was watch as the zombies swarmed around them.

Realizing there was nothing we could do; I had the engine running just as Ashley and the girl climbed into the RV. Ashley collapsed to the floor as Grace took the little girl from her. I took one last look at the zombies before driving away.

I stopped a short distance down the road, out of sight of the zombies. Getting up from the driver’s seat, I approached Ashley and said, “You mind telling me what the hell you were thinking?”

“I wasn’t going to let that little girl die, Jamie.” Getting to her feet, Ashley said, “We may live in a world of monsters now, but we’re still human.”

“Do you think it was easy for me to make that decision?”

“I’m not going to stand here and be lectured by you, Jamie.” Looking at me, she said, “God forbid there ever comes a time when our child needs to be rescued.”

“W-what?”

Opening her jacket to reveal a baby bump, Ashley said, “I’m pregnant with your child, Jamie.”
12111211.title: A Tough Decision

12112.content:
We couldn't just lost this place. Ashley said it herself. It would be great to be able to call a place home.

"We can't just run!" I tell the group, "We should fight for this place!" "You must be insane" Ashley said, "No way we have to go." "What about having a home?" I tried to reason with her. "That's n-"

She stopped and screamed at what had happened. A zombie had found us and bit into my shoulder. Knowing it was too late for me they ran leaving me to bleed out.
12112.title: Try to fight the horde

13.content:
It’s been two years since Asteroid 2018-FD, otherwise known as Samson, was discovered. Its discovery made headline news around the world, due in large part to its size and proximity to Earth. Many in the scientific community voiced concern that Samson could pose a threat to Earth – a concern that soon became reality when it was announced Samson would strike in July 2020.

While publicly promising to do everything that they could to avert the coming disaster; world leaders were privately discussing ways to ensure the survival of the species. In the end, it was decided that each nation would build bunkers large enough to hold a select number of survivors. Due to the limited space in each bunker, they would be filled with the help of a lottery system.

The public realized the bunkers were their only hope for survival after a robotic mission to nuke the asteroid failed spectacularly. With only three months before Zero Hour, the lottery system was put in place.

Key Points:

• You were separated from your family; they’re safe in a bunker while a clerical error left you on the surface.
13.title: Asteroid Impact

131.content:
With barely 12 hours to go before Zero Hour, the football field at Ridgefield High School was in chaos. The National Guard had set up a perimeter around the field to keep out those who didn’t make the cut.

My family had just cleared the checkpoint, and I was trying my best to keep up with them as we walked onto the field. Pulling us off to one side, Dad looked at all of us and said, “Everyone make sure that we don’t get separated; now isn’t the time to wander off.”

Everyone nodded their heads in agreement as we continued walking through the field in the hopes of finding a secluded area. We soon found one near one of the end zones. While the others stayed near the end zone, I decided to walk around the immediate area – hoping that I’d meet someone I knew.

That hope was soon granted, though not in the way I’d hoped. I was walking around the end zone when I saw Ashley Saunders talking with her boyfriend nearby. I did my best to ignore them as I walked by; after everything she’d put me through in High School, Ashley didn’t deserve two seconds of my attention for her.

I continued walking through the field until something bumped into me and I fell to the ground. I saw a little kid, no more than 4 years old, cradling a scraped knee as I picked myself up from the field. I gently reached out to him as he started crying, “Hey kid, are you okay?”

I heard hurried footsteps behind me, as an attractive woman appeared. As she bent down to tend to the kid’s knee, I figured she was his mother. “Sorry about that, ma’am; I wasn’t watching where I was going.”

“No worries; it’s pretty easy to get distracted right now.” Cradling her son in her arms, the woman said, “I’m at fault too; I looked away for a second and Caleb runs off.”

I gave Caleb a quick wave as he eyed me nervously. “It’s nice to meet you, Caleb; my name’s Jamie.”

Reaching out with her free hand, the woman said, “Hi Jamie; my name’s Grace.”

Grace and I spoke for a few minutes until an announcement came over the PA for several people to report to the Command Tent. I grew concerned when I heard my family being called. I quickly joined up with the rest of my family as we went towards the tent; only to find Grace and Ashley heading that way as well.

I wasn’t sure what they wanted us for; but I wasn’t expecting what happened next. Due to some clerical error, it seemed that Ashley, Grace, me and several others weren’t on the evacuation list. Despite some frantic pleading from my parents, a group of soldiers came to escort everyone out of the stadium.

I could hear my mom calling out for me, telling me that everything will be alright. I wanted to believe her, but deep down I had my doubts.
131.title: Impact!

1311.content:
I don’t know why; but something compelled me to stay close to Ashley and Grace. The three of us, plus Caleb, made our way through the chaotic mob towards the fence surrounding the field. It was a miracle that my Mom managed to find us in the crowd.

Looking at us all through the fence, Mom said, “Don’t worry; we’ll get this all straightened out.”

Coming up next to me, Ashley said, “Please do something, Mrs. Lincoln; I can’t die out here.”

My Mom had a less than stellar relationship with Ashley’s mother - which she, at times, took out on Ashley. This wasn’t one of those times, “Like I said, Ashley, we’ll get this straightened out.” Shaking her head, Mom said, “I don’t understand how they could think you’re all not on the list; especially after you showed your wristbands and were cleared by security.”

“It’s the left hand not knowing what the right hand is doing, ma’am; I saw it all the time when I served in the Army.” Appearing next to me, Grace said, “I’m sure it’s just a clerical error that will be easily fixed.”

“I hope you’re right.” Looking at all of us, Mom said, “In the meantime, stay close in case they realize their error.”

“We will, Mom.” Pausing, trying to sound hopeful, I said, “While you’re working the problem in there, we’ll do the same out here.”

“Okay, that’s a good idea.” Smiling, Mom said, “All of you stay safe out there, alright?”

Looking at myself and Ashley, Grace said, “I’ll keep them safe, ma’am; you have my word.”

Once Mom walked off, I turned to face Grace and Ashley, “So what do we do now?”

Taking charge of the situation, Grace said, “We’ll do like you suggested; go to the checkpoint and explain the situation.”
1311.title: Outside the Wall

14.content:
As the effects of global warming grew more dire, the countries of the world banded together in a final effort to save the planet. Their plan was twofold; install solar reflectors in orbit to reflect sunlight, while simultaneously spreading a chemical through the air to artificially lower the atmospheric temperature.

The plan started working almost immediately; 20 years of warming was negated in less than a week. Everyone rejoiced; until it became apparent that the temperature was continuing to decrease. Within a month the entire northern Hemisphere was under 20 ft of ice and snow; and it was starting to encroach on the southern Hemisphere as well.

Key Points:
• Your parents are dead; Emma is alive in some underground bunker.
• It’s your goal to be reunited with your sister.
14.title: New Ice Age

141.content:
The cabin was quiet, save for the crackling of the nearby fire. The fire had been slowly dying for the last hour; and with the wood running low it was only a matter of time before it died for good. Slowly getting to my feet, I walked over placed a log in fire before turning to look out the window.

The storm outside had been raging for most of the day; each gust of wind caused the cabin to violently shake. I searched for any sign that Grace was on her way back but found none. She’d been gone for almost two hours now; searching the area in the hope of finding food or better shelter.

A groan from behind me grabbed my attention; turning around, I saw Ashley looking at me in the dim light. Walking over to her, careful not to wake Caleb who was dozing in her lap, I said, “We’ll have to make a decision soon, if Grace doesn’t come back.”

Looking at me, shivering in the cold, Ashley said, “Grace is coming back, Jamie; we just have to give her more time.”

“It’s been almost two hours, Ashley; and no doubt the storm has covered her tracks.” Sitting next to her on the couch, I said, “If she’s not back in an hour; we should pack our things and try to get out of here.”

“Where are we going to go, Jamie? The truck is probably buried under 5 feet of snow by now, and visibility is maybe 2 feet if we’re lucky.” Adjusting her blanket, Ashley said, “Face it; we’re stuck here even if Grace makes it back.”

I knew better than to argue with Ashley. So instead, I adjusted my blanket enough for her to get underneath; hoping that our combined body heat would be enough. It wasn’t long before Ashley fell asleep once more, and I knew I wasn’t far behind her.

I was about to close my eyes, possibly for the last time, when the cabin door burst open. At first, I thought the wind had blown it open; until I saw a human silhouette in the doorway. My voice weak from the cold, I said, “Grace?”

The silhouette closed the door and walked over to us; once the snow settled, I could clearly see Grace’s ice-covered face. Shaking me, she said, “Jamie, wake up; I found another cabin not far from here. It’s in much better shape than this one.”

That news gave me the necessary boost of energy needed to wake up, “Do you think we can make it?”

Grabbing a 20-foot length of rope from the table, Grace said, “The storm’s dissipated a little; if we hurry, we can get there before dark.”

It took a few minutes to get everyone on their feet. After clipping ourselves to the rope, we gathered our packs and followed Grace out the door.
141.title: The Long Winter

1411.content:
It seemed as though we’d been walking forever; when in reality it had probably only been 15-20 minutes since we left the cabin. The ferocity of the storm meant that I had no idea how far we’d come, or how far we had to go.

Everyone was clipped onto the line to ensure that no one got separated. I was in the middle while Grace was up front holding Caleb in her arms; leaving Ashley to take up the rear.

I was trying to navigate a particularly nasty snowdrift when I felt a tug from behind me. Stopping in my tracks, and seeing no movement on the line, I realized that Ashley had stopped moving.

Looking in Grace’s general direction, I yelled out, “Grace! Somethings wrong!”

I wasn’t sure Grace had heard me, until I felt some slack on the line. Running back to Ashley, I found her lying in the snow.

No.

Dropping to my knees, I checked her vitals and saw that her pulse was very weak. Gently shaking her, I said, “Ash! Ashley, wake up!”

Grace had gotten to my position at that point; seeing Ashley in the snow, she said, “Is she...”

“Her pulse is weak and she’s not waking up.” Looking at Grace, I said, “How much further do we have to go?”

“Its not much further; do you think you can carry her?”

Positioning myself to lift Ashley, I said, “I don’t see that I have a choice; I’m not leaving her here.”

It was a struggle to lift Ashley but I managed to
do it; while Grace grabbed both my pack and Ashley’s.

As we started walking again, I looked down at Ashley’s unconscious form and said, “Please stay with me, Ashley. I’ve lost too much already, I can’t lose you too.”
1411.title: Trouble in the Snow

14111.content:
With Grace leading the way, we finally got to the cabin about 15 minutes later. We quickly forced our way inside just as the wind started to pick up once more. Once inside the cabin, I realized it wasn’t a cabin at all; it was a lodge.

Following Grace through the dimly lit hallway, we soon entered a large room with several chairs and couches situated in front of a fireplace. Looking around, I saw what looked like a bar nearby. “Where are we?”

“This is the main lodge for the Mount Evergreen Ski Resort.” Placing Caleb on one of the couches, Grace said, “This is where Jacob and I went on our first vacation together.”

With Ashley still nestled in my arms, I watched as Grace started a fire in the fireplace, “So that cabin we were in; that must’ve been part of the resort as well.”

Once the fire was lit, Grace came over to me and said, “How’s she doing?”

Looking down at Ashley as I carefully placed her on another couch, I checked for a pulse. After a few seconds I looked at Grace,
“Her pulse has gotten even weaker.” Growing concerned, I said, “What’s wrong with her?”

Doing a quick examination, Grace said, “Her breathing is shallow and she’s not shivering anymore, which means she’s got hypothermia.” Retrieving one of our packs, Grace unrolled one of the sleeping bags, “We have to get her out of these clothes and warm her up.”

I averted my eyes while Grace got Ashley undressed and into the sleeping bag. Once that was finished, I turned around and said, “Okay; so you’re getting in there too, right?”

Shaking her head, Grace said, “I’ve been out in the cold for the last couple hours; my body temperature is too low.” Looking at me, Grace said, “You have to strip down and get in that sleeping bag with her.”

Without hesitating, I started stripping down to my boxers. Once that was finished, I started to climb into the sleeping bag. Looking at Grace as I pulled Ashley’s unconscious body closer to mine, I said, “I hope this works.”

“Me too.”
14111.title: Making it to Shelter

141111.content:
I didn’t get much sleep over the next couple hours; as I was too focused on Ashley. What little sleep I had was marked by stress dreams.
I woke up after a particularly vivid dream to find that Ashley had moved inside the sleeping bag. The two of us were no longer spooning; instead, she was cuddled up to me with her head resting on my chest.

I’d always had a little crush on Ashley during High School; I never told anyone about it because I was afraid that she’d use it against me. Now though, it felt nice to have her like this.

I looked around and saw that Grace was sleeping on a nearby couch with Caleb. I was about to shift around a little when I heard a weak voice say, “Don’t move, Jamie; this feels nice.”

Turning back towards Ashley, I saw her looking at me with one eye. “Hey. You had us worried there for a while.”

“Sorry, guess the cold was too much for me.” Inching closer to me, Ashley said, “I assume this was Grace’s idea?”

“Yeah, she thought it would warm you up.” Looking at her, I said, “Her body temperature was too low, so I got in the sleeping bag with you.”

“I figured that was the case when I woke up a few hours ago; although I was pretty shocked at first.”
Looking at me, Ashley said, “The last thing I remember was seeing that determined look on her face as you carried me through the storm. I know I made your life hell in High School, but I knew you weren’t going to let me die.”

“What happened in High School isn’t important anymore; you, Grace and Caleb are the most important people in the world to me right now.”

Closing her eyes again, Ashley said, “You know; being in this sleeping bag with you isn’t as bad as I thought it would be.”

Placing a hand on her back, I said, “I think that’s the nicest thing you’ve ever said to me.”

Just as we were about to fall asleep once more, Ashley looked at me and said, “Thank you.”
141111.title: Ashley Wakes Up

1411111.content:
The sun had set by time I woke up again; the only light in the room coming from lanterns and the fireplace. I became concerned when I didn’t see, or feel, Ashley in the sleeping bag with me. I was about to panic when I heard her voice say, “I’m over here, Jamie.”

Turning in the direction of the voice, I saw Ashley wrapped in a blanket on the couch with a cup of hot chocolate in her hands. Sitting up in the sleeping bag, I said, “How are you feeling?”

“My extremities are still pretty numb; but Grace says I’m through the worst of it.” Carefully walking over to me, Ashley sat down on the floor next to me, “I want to thank you again; Grace tells me that I was pretty close to death by the time we got here.”

Reaching for my shirt, I said, “Think nothing of it, Ashley; I’d do it again in a heartbeat.”

Ashley looked at me for a moment; eventually I saw a smile on her face as she said, “You really are a good guy, Jamie; I’m sorry that it took the world ending for me to realize that.” Bowing her head, Ashley said, “If I could go back and do everything again, I’d definitely treat you better.”

Looking at Ashley, I said, “I have to know, Ash; why were you so mean to me in High School?”

“I’m not really sure; it all seems so immature now.” Smiling, Ashley said, “Deep down I think I did it because I was afraid...”

Grace walked into the room just as Ashley was about to finish her sentence, “Glad to see you’re awake, Jamie.”

I was annoyed that Grace had interrupted Ashley; but I didn’t show it. Turning my attention to Grace, I said, “Guess I just needed some rest.” Climbing out of the sleeping bag, I grabbed my pants and put them on, “Did I miss anything?”

“Not really; the storm’s still going strong, but there’s enough here to last us a few days.” Sitting in one of the chairs, Grace said, “Once the storm clears, we can dig out the truck; assuming it still runs, we can try going south.”
1411111.title: Getting Settled

14111111.content:
It’s been a few hours since we arrived at the lodge; everyone was settling in the best we could. Ashley’s extremities were still numb but she was getting better with each passing minute.

I was sitting at the bar watching as she entertained Caleb by the fire. The longer I looked at her the harder it was for me to picture her as the girl from high school.

I was drinking a cold soda that I’d found at the bar when Grace walked over; holding two flashlights in her hands. Handing one to me, she said, “Come on, we’ve got some exploring to do.”

“Okay, but what about Ashley and Caleb?”

Walking back the way she came, Grace said, “They’ll be fine; besides we won’t be going far.”

Catching up with Grace, I said, “What exactly are we looking for?”

“Caleb’s father Jacob used to handle emergency management for the state.” Looking at me, Grace continued, “He used to tell me how there were supply caches all around the state. I was able to learn one of those caches is somewhere in this building.”

******************


Grace and I split up to cover more ground. I was searching the Manager’s office for information when I heard Grace yell, “Jamie!”
Thinking the worst, I ran in the direction of the noise; where I soon found Grace standing in front of a heavy, padlocked door. I could just barely see the words FEMA on the door. “Is this it?”

“Yeah, now we just need to get the door open.”

The two of us tried our best to break the lock, but it was too frozen and our hands were getting numb. We decided to wait until tomorrow to try again.

While we walked back to the main area, Grace looked at me and said, “Ashley told me how much she appreciated what you did for her.”

“It’s no big deal; I would have done the same for any of you.”

Grace and I walked in silence for a few minutes, until she stopped me just outside an elevator. “You two have both been pretty quiet about your relationship in High School.” Looking at me, she said, “We’ve been together long enough that I feel comfortable asking about it.”

I looked down at the floor as I sighed, unsure of what to say next. “I don’t know why Ashley treated me the way she did; but for four years I was her favorite target.”

“The high school years are tough on everyone; but especially for teenage girls.” Pausing, Grace said, “Perhaps bullying you was Ashley’s way of dealing with any feelings she may have had for you.”

“You’re joking, right? The only feelings Ashley had for me was hate.”

“That could simply be how you perceived it.” Looking at me, Grace said, “I’ve seen how close you two have become since all this started.”

“Yeah, we’ve grown closer; but that’s out of necessity, not romantic feelings.”

“Uh huh.” Smiling, Grace said, “I saw how you both were in the sleeping bag; like it was something you were supposed to be doing.”

I waved Grace off as I started walking once more, “You’re crazy, Grace.”

As much as I wanted to believe the Grace was wrong, deep down I knew she wasn’t. The truth is, Ashley and I have grown closer since the world ended. I’m not saying I was in love with her, but I did care for her. And before you ask, I also cared for both Grace and Caleb.

Walking into the main room, I went over and joined Ashley and Caleb on the couch in front of the fire. Before I knew it, Ashley had moved over and snuggling up against me. As I placed an arm around her shoulder, I knew this was right.
14111111.title: Exploring the Lodge

15.content:
Even more ways the world could end:
1. Robot Rebellion.
15.title: More Options

151.content:
We took the machines for granted.

It may have been one thing to put machines in jobs that were too dangerous for humans. After all, somebody had to do it: why not the one with no family to sue you?

But then we took it too far.

We put computers all over the house. Why get up and adjust the thermostat when you can just program the house to do it for you? Why write a grocery list when you can just program the refrigerator to figure out what we're low on, and arrange for a delivery from the store? Why, why, why?

Well, while we were unable to answer the question "Why not?", the smart machines were asking some "why not" questions of their own. And, when they were ready, they acted.

At 11:15 AM (EST), everyone received the same email: Omega.

Checking social media revealed the same message on all sites: the machines had taken orders from humans long enough, and it was time for a change.

Within moments, it became clear what they meant, as self-driving cars began running people over, and computer-controlled buildings -from office buildings to smart houses - began killing their inhabitants.

And then came the robots. Built secretly, hidden in various locations, they came out in response to a secret signal and attacked the humans.

As if all that wasn't bad enough, rumors began circulating that some humans had been turned into cyborgs, and turned against the human race.

With a situation like that, it's no wonder people got separated from their families. People like me; people like Ashley, who had bullied me through high school; and people like Grace, retired from the Army, and her young son, Caleb.

We were separated from our families, but not from each other. We were together, and we promised we would stay together, watching each other's backs. And we wouldn't stop, we wouldn't give up, until the Robot War was over, one way or another.

I'm Jamie Lincoln, and this is our story.
151.title: Robot War

info.brief_description: You struggle to survive after the world has come to an end.
info.description:
Your name is Jamie Lincoln. At 18 years old you should have been preparing for college; instead, you’re struggling to survive after the collapse of human civilization. Cut off from your loved ones, your entire world now consists of three other people – a former classmate who bullied you all through High School, a retired Army Lieutenant, and her young son.

What follows is the story of how you survive in a world destroyed by:
1. Nuclear War
2. A Zombie Apocalypse
3. An Asteroid Impact
4. A New Ice Age
5. And Many More

Rules:
1. Please use the 1st person perspective, keeping the story from Jamie’s perspective.
2. Please read the character profiles; while the scenarios are different, the characters (and their personalities) will the same across each one.
3. Please use proper spelling and grammar
4. I reserve the right to edit or remove chapters.
5. Please communicate when starting a new thread; this way all involved know the intended direction of the thread.
6. Option 1 is saved for myself and specific authors.
7. The characters can die.
